externalExamBoundary: Pinned freeCodeCamp v9 evidence exposes only a 296-byte pointer to an external exam environment. It exposes no reviewable items, blueprint, scoring, accessibility, security, pilot, or standard-setting evidence, so this original blueprint grants it zero item-level or validity credit.

exam.title: Responsive Web Design Evidence and Transfer Examination
exam.correctionPolicy: A failed attempt reveals strand-level evidence gaps but never secure items or canonical answers. Learner completes targeted worked examples, parallel corrective tasks, and delayed retrieval before receiving a different balanced secure form.

strands.title: HTML content, source, tooling, documents, and links
strands.validityClaim: Learner can author meaningful HTML content, distinguish source from parsed output, operate the project safely, trace document loading, and resolve purposeful links under changed paths.

strands.title: HTML images, media, text semantics, and page structure
strands.validityClaim: Learner can select semantic image, media, inline-text, landmark, section, article, and disclosure structures while defending alternatives, rights, equivalence, and failure behavior.

strands.title: HTML forms, recovery, and data tables
strands.validityClaim: Learner can model submitted data and two-dimensional relationships, implement native controls and headers, and preserve labels, valid data, errors, correction, and table context.

strands.title: HTML accessibility evidence, diagnosis, and transfer
strands.validityClaim: Learner can reason from people and barriers through DOM and accessibility evidence, diagnose interacting HTML failures, protect changed cases, and defend an independent semantic service.

strands.title: CSS language, selectors, cascade, and reusable values
strands.validityClaim: Learner can connect stylesheet source to computed outcomes, predict matched sets and cascade provenance, repair conflicts, and design reusable custom-property contracts without token-only evidence.

strands.title: CSS boxes, sizing, overflow, paint, and transforms
strands.validityClaim: Learner can explain and measure display, box geometry, intrinsic constraints, units, overflow, logical dimensions, paint layers, and transforms across changed content and containing blocks.

strands.title: CSS font delivery, typography, color, and visual effects
strands.validityClaim: Learner can deliver resilient fonts, compose readable text, build semantic color roles, and verify contrast, language, fallback, filter, gradient, and rendering-stability behavior.

strands.title: User evidence, prototypes, design systems, and components
strands.validityClaim: Learner can translate user and stakeholder evidence into task flows, prototypes, revisions, hierarchy, tokens, components, states, progressive enhancement, and defensible handoff decisions.

strands.title: Flex, Grid, positioning, stacking, anchors, and floats
strands.validityClaim: Learner can choose and trace one- and two-dimensional layout, free space, tracks, placement, reference frames, stacking contexts, anchor fallbacks, and legitimate float wrapping without breaking source order.

strands.title: Fluid responsive pages, components, media, and navigation
strands.validityClaim: Learner can build fluid defaults, derive content breakpoints, distinguish viewport from container adaptation, select media, preserve complete navigation, and execute a continuous-size evidence matrix.

strands.title: Accessible interaction, workflows, preferences, output, and motion
strands.validityClaim: Learner can preserve tasks under keyboard, touch, zoom, focus, dialog, recovery, forced-colors, print, reduced-motion, transition, animation, and interruption conditions.

strands.title: CSS diagnostics, performance, regression, and independent transfer
strands.validityClaim: Learner can diagnose cascade-to-render failures, measure stability and performance, protect changed cases, and independently research, build, test, revise, and defend a responsive stakeholder product.

randomizationBoundaries: Randomize within reviewed strand, cognitive-demand, evidence-mode, dependency, and difficulty cells; never sample unrelated items merely to hit a count.
Keep scenario assets, answer options, hidden changed cases, and performance-task variants together so randomization cannot invalidate evidence or accessibility.
Balance every form to the exact strand and cognitive blueprint before delivery; a random seed cannot override prerequisite, form-equivalence, or exposure rules.
Do not present two items whose answer or failure output reveals another secure item in the same form.
exposureControls: Keep item prompts, canonical answers, hidden checks, scoring keys, and unused variants server-side; client submissions remain evidence only.
Retire or quarantine exposed, statistically unstable, technically stale, biased, or accessibility-defective items and rebuild affected forms before reuse.
Issue a different reviewed form after correction; do not repeat failed secure items until an approved delayed-retention policy permits it.
Record form, item revision, source version, attempt, hints, runtime, accessibility accommodation, and scoring revision without storing unnecessary personal data.

scoring.credentialRule: No credential is issued from an arbitrary percentage. Learner must pass all five independently reviewed projects, satisfy each critical strand and safety/accessibility requirement, and meet a defensible exam cut score established only after review and pilot evidence.
standardSettingPlan: Independent subject, instructional, assessment, accessibility, and practitioner reviewers classify every item by competency, demand, evidence mode, essentiality, and likely minimally competent performance.
Run a documented modified-Angoff study with trained panelists after item review and representative pilot data; record ratings, discussion, revisions, convergence, and uncertainty.
Compare recommended thresholds with contrasting-groups and decision-consistency evidence where sample size and ethics permit; do not tune the standard to a desired pass rate.
Set critical-strand and project rules separately where compensating one severe accessibility, semantic, or behavior failure with unrelated recall would invalidate the credential claim.
Re-run standard setting after material scope, runtime, form, scoring, accessibility, or item-bank changes.
itemAnalysisPlan: Estimate item difficulty, discrimination, omitted-response patterns, time, hint or correction history, and distractor functioning by form and relevant learner groups.
Review differential performance only with lawful, ethical, adequately sized data and qualitative evidence; never infer learner ability from demographic differences alone.
Investigate local dependence, speededness, form imbalance, compromised exposure, runtime defects, and accessibility barriers before interpreting score differences.
Quarantine items with negative discrimination, broken distractors, ambiguous scoring, technical defects, construct-irrelevant reading load, or accessibility failures pending review.
Report score precision, classification consistency, form-equating limits, missing data, accommodations, and residual validity risks with every credential decision.

accessibilityAndSecurity: All item types must be keyboard-operable, screen-reader coherent, zoom/reflow safe, target-size compliant, non-color-dependent, and tested with reduced motion and forced colors where relevant.
Performance tasks run only in the isolated browser workspace with deterministic assets, bounded resources, sandboxed preview, and no learner source execution on the application host.
Assessment timing must support approved accommodations and pause/recovery without changing the construct or leaking secure content; speed is not scored unless explicitly validated.
Canonical answers, hidden changed cases, rubrics, form assemblies, and cut-score material remain server-side with least-privilege access and auditable revisions.
Every item receives content, plain-language, bias/sensitivity, accessibility, runtime, and answer-key review before pilot; automated scans never substitute for task testing.
Failure, reconnect, autosave, resume, focus return, status announcement, and evidence preservation must work without granting or denying credit from infrastructure failure.

No camera, microphone, biometric, invasive proctoring, or unrelated personal-data collection is assumed; any future integrity control requires separate necessity, privacy, accessibility, and learner review.
requiredReviewEvidence: Complete trace from every item and performance-task rubric to one or more researched concepts, target demand, evidence mode, source version, and terminal credential claim.
Independent review signatures and resolved findings for subject accuracy, instructional fit, assessment validity, accessibility, bias/sensitivity, security, runtime, and originality.
Representative pilot records for task comprehension, completion, errors, abandonment, accommodations, time, technical failures, and correction without publishing secure content.
Form-level evidence for strand balance, cognitive balance, parallel difficulty, local dependence, exposure, score precision, and classification consistency.
Documented standard-setting study, rationale, uncertainty, critical-strand rules, retest policy, appeals path, and scheduled revalidation triggers.
Semantic duplication audit across prompts, scenarios, options, starters, checks, rubrics, feedback, correction tasks, and all secure forms.
Observed learner transfer evidence from all five projects plus unfamiliar exam tasks; guided completion or source-shape matches cannot validate the credential.
gaps: No assessment items, secure forms, performance-task variants, scoring rubrics, pilot responses, item statistics, cut score, form-equating evidence, or standard-setting panel evidence exist yet.
Seven unresolved course concepts and seven unapproved modern extensions must close or be removed before final blueprint approval.
Independent subject, instructional, assessment, accessibility, security, privacy, originality, runtime, and representative-learner reviews remain open.
Credential issuance stays blocked until projects, item banks, forms, scoring, correction, accessibility, security, pilots, and standard setting pass.
